# Introduction Vex is a Creative, Responsive Material Design Admin Template built with Angular 12+ and the Angular-CLI. It extends the Material Design components built by the Angular team and it offers you everything you need to get started with your next CRM, CMS, Project Management, or other projects. Vex has **no dependency on jQuery or similiar libraries**, Angular's functionality is completely used. Support is available through E-Mail ([themeforest@visurel.com](mailto:themeforest@visurel.com)). If you purchased the theme and love it, consider giving it a 5-star rating here on ThemeForest. It really helps pushing out more updates and adding more great features. # Getting Started > In this section you will find the basic folder structure and everything you need to get the template up and running the first time to start developing. ## Folder Structure | Name | Description | | -------------: |--------------- | `angular.json` | Used for configuration of project specific settings. You can add external styles and scripts, change the output folder, add assets, add environment files and more. | | `e2e` | Will be used for end-to-end tests to ensure functionality for users before deploying. | | `node_modules` | All external modules used are here. Do not mess with this folder, as it is auto-generated by using `npm install`. | | `package.json` | Contains all dependencies used for production and development. | | `src` | Contains all Angular Typescript code, assets and basically everything the end user will have access to. | | `tslint.json` | Angular-CLI includes an automatic Typescript-Linter, which can be configured with this file. | ## Installation Angular-CLI allows you to create a new App in a matter of seconds and provides an awesome way to generate scaffolds for basically all Angular-Components. [You can take a look at what commands are available here.](//github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/master/packages/angular/cli/README.md#generating-components-directives-pipes-and-services) In this section, we are going to install Angular-CLI and it's prerequisites and then generate our first project with a few various components. ### Prerequisites > Before we can install Angular-CLI we will have to get a few thing set up first. To run Angular-CLI we will need to install this prerequisite first: * **NodeJS** v10 or newer [A detailed instruction on how to install NodeJS is available here.](//docs.npmjs.com/getting-started/installing-node) ### Installing Angular-CLI Installing Angular-CLI is as simple as running this simple command: `npm install -g @angular/cli@latest` or `sudo npm install -g @angular/cli@latest` and the package manager `npm` will do the rest. ### Install Vex Dependencies Navigate to the Vex folder and run `npm install` to install all dependencies required by Vex. ## Start Development Server Run `ng serve` for a dev server. Navigate to `http://localhost:4200/`. The app will automatically reload if you change any of the source files. ## Build for Production If you want to create a build for a production environment you can simply run `npm run build` or `ng build --prod` and you will get static HTML and JS files in the `/dist` folder ready to be uploaded to any server. # Customization In this section you are going to learn how to customize Vex to be exactly the way you want it to be. ## Configuration Configuring the Vex Layout to your needs is as easy as setting a simple object with the values you want, here's an example configuration which handles everything for you: You can change these values even at run-time and the page will adjust to the changes. ```typescript { id: 'vex-default', layout: 'horizontal', boxed: false, sidenav: { layout: 'expanded' }, toolbar: { fixed: true }, footer: { visible: true, fixed: true } } ``` ## Changing Styling and CSS Variables > Most of the styling (padding, colors, fonts, font-weights, ...) is build very modular and easily customizable. If you want to change any specific style globally you can just change the CSS Variable inside the `style.scss` file and it will update every single section of the page. Here are just a few example variables, almost everything is done through variables: ```typescript --container-width: 1200px; --padding: 24px; --font: Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; --font-weight-semi-bold: 500; --text-color: #{$dark-primary-text}; --sidenav-width: 280px; --sidenav-background: #{$sidenav-background}; --sidenav-color: white; // and much more... ``` ## Using Custom Colors for the Primary/Secondary/Warn Palettes > Inside the `style.scss` find the section below. The values behind the names are just `rgb` colors, so `--color-primary` would be `rgb(92, 119, 255)` (92, 119, 255) (just without the rgb()) if you want to customize these colors you can do that easily by changing the colors for these variables. For each `primary color` there is a `contrast` variable. The contrast variable is used when you want to display something on the `primary color`. Example: You use `--color-primary` as your background color (and the color is pretty dark), and want to display text on that background, then you want the `--color-primary-contrast` to be `white` and use that as the text color. This works the same for all the other colors too. ```typescript // Colors (style.scss) --color-primary: 92, 119, 255; --color-primary-contrast: 255, 255, 255; --color-accent: 255, 64, 129; --color-accent-contrast: 255, 255, 255; --color-warn: 244, 67, 54; --color-warn-contrast: 255, 255, 255; ``` ## Adding Menu Items Our Menu is generated completely dynamic and is therefore very easy to customize and use, the simplest way is to simply inject the NavigationService and just override the `items` instance variable. Navigation Items have the `NavigationItem` type which you can use for autocompletion and type-safety. (Generally, all our components have typings available!) You can add 3 different types: **Subheading, Link, Dropdown** Here's an example inside the AppComponent: ```typescript @Component({ selector: 'vex-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.scss'] }) export class AppComponent { title = 'vex'; constructor(private navigationService: NavigationService) { this.navigationService.items = [ { label: 'Subheading' }, { label: 'Item with a Link', route: '/my-link', icon: icAssigment }, { label: 'Dropdown Item', icon: icAdd, children: [ { label: 'Item with Link inside Dropdown', route: '/custom-link' } ] } ]; } } ``` ## Generating the first `Component` > Now that we have installed all prerequisites it's time to start developing our app. Angular-CLI offers a lot of assistance and allows you to generate basically all Angular-Components there are. *(In a smart way!)* To generate our first `component` we simply open up a terminal and navigate in our Angular-App. Now we simply run `ng g component client` and we get a new component in `/src/app/client` with the following files: * `client.component.ts` * `client.component.html` * `client.component.css` * `client.component.spec.ts` The files `client.component.ts` and `client.component.spec.ts` contain the most code, the other files only contain placeholders. **client.component.ts** ```typescript import { Component, OnInit } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-client', templateUrl: 'client.component.html', styleUrls: ['client.component.css'] }) export class ClientComponent implements OnInit { constructor() { } ngOnInit() { } } ``` By executing this short command, we just saved ourselves a lot of time creating all these `Component` files and boilerplate code. [Syntax for all commands are available here.](//github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/master/packages/angular/cli/README.md#generating-components-directives-pipes-and-services) ## Generating a Service in a specific folder Now we have our `component`, but what if we want to share some data between components and need to create a `service` to manage this all. Well, we probably would want the service to be in the correct folder, either right in the components folder or in the `shared` folder in `/src/app/`. Either way, with Angular-CLI we can generate in any folder, wherever we want. Simply use the path *(relative to `/src/app/`)* and use it as the name of the generated component. `ng g service shared/client` or `ng g service client/shared/client` Or anything you need. Afterward we will find two generated files in our specified folder: `client.service.ts` and `client.service.spec.ts`. **client.service.ts** ```typescript import { Injectable } from '@angular/core'; @Injectable() export class ClientService { constructor() { } } ``` ## Running unit tests Run `ng test` to execute the unit tests via [Karma](https://karma-runner.github.io). ## Running end-to-end tests Run `ng e2e` to execute the end-to-end tests via [Protractor](http://www.protractortest.org/). ## Further help > If you have any specific questions about the template, you can contact us anytime on our support email ([themeforest@visurel.com](mailto:themeforest@visurel.com)) and we'll help you with any issues you may encounter. If you encounter any bugs or issues, feel free to send them over to us with a detailed description and we'll fix the issue ASAP. Thanks for reading, if you have any pre-sale questions or just anything, don't hesitate to contact us! :)

VEX

# Changelog ## 12.0.0 (2021-06-21) - Upgrade to Angular ^12.0 - Upgrade all dependencies to latest compatible versions - Adjust SCSS for @angular/material changes ## 11.1.0 (2021-03-12) - Upgrade to Angular ^11.2 - Upgrade all dependencies to latest compatible versions - Simplify TailwindCSS setup ## 11.0.0 (2020-11-12) - Upgrade to Angular 11+ - Upgrade all dependencies to latest compatible versions ## 10.0.2 (2020-10-01) - Upgrade all dependencies to latest versions ## 10.0.0 (2020-07-06) ### Features - Upgrade to Angular 10+ - Upgrade all dependencies to latest compatible versions ### Breaking Changes We switched from ngx-take-until-destroy to ngneat/until-destroy (the successor of the first library available for Angular 10+) and there are adjustments needed. There's an easy migration script: 1. `cd src` (into your /src folder in the vex-angular folder) 2. run `npx @ngneat/until-destroy --removeOnDestroy` A few occurences could still appear, because the automated script can't pick them up. Here's a step-by-step on how to manually adjust the entries: Replace: `import { untilDestroyed } from 'ngx-take-until-destroy';` With: `import { UntilDestroy, untilDestroyed } from '@ngneat/until-destroy';` and add `@UntilDestroy()` above your `@Component()` or `@Injectable()` Example: ```typescript @UntilDestroy() @Injectable() export class TestService { test() { test$.pipe( untilDestroyed(this) ).subscribe(value => doStuff(value)); } } ``` This library and structure is needed so any subscriptions we create get automatically cleaned up (unsubscribed) when the component/service gets destroyed by Angular. ## 9.2.0 (2020-06-03) ### Features - Add [Mailbox](/apps/mail) - Improve letter-spacing/line-height of Typography for best readability: [Inter Dynamic Metrics](https://rsms.me/inter/dynmetrics/) ### Fixes - Load icons on icons demo page deferred to improve build time when developing ## 9.1.0 (2020-05-13) ### Features - Add [Social/Timeline](/apps/social/timeline) page - Simplify color customization, simply change the CSS Variables - Add Progress Bar indicating lazy loaded routes being loaded - Changing direction (RTL/LTR) now doesn't require a reload and can be changed anytime - Upgrade dependencies - Change Font Family ### Fixes - Sidenav now hidden correctly on Hermes/Ikaros (Vertical Layouts) - Input Dropdown Icon now correctly aligned (vertical & horizontal) - Ares Layout: Navigation active color correctly used now ## 9.0.0 (2020-03-24) ### Features - Upgrade to Angular 9+ - Upgrade to Angular Material 9+ - Upgrade all dependencies to be compatible with Angular 9 - Upgrade to TypeScript 3.7.5 ## 8.4.0 (2019-11-18) ### Features - Add CustomLayoutComponent to easily create your own custom layout component and customize the template even easier - If needed, you can now enable strictFunctionTypes & noImplicitReturns in your tsconfig.json - Add more customization options using NavigationService (Change title & logo in sidenav) - Add example config with customized completely customized layout and custom title & logo ## 8.3.0 (2019-10-22) ### Features - Add global search (click on the search button in toolbar) - Add WYSIWYG Editor - Add more custom columns to All-In-One Table ### Fixes - Improve dark mode styles, especially color usage is using opacity now - Contact Table is now fully responsive - General style improvements ## 8.2.0 (2019-10-07) ### Features - Add Typings for TailwindCSS, use TailwindCSS configuration inside your Angular application - 1 Configuration file! ### Fixes - Fix minor issue on mobile devices, dashboard was overflowing and forced a horizontal scroll on some devices ## 8.1.0 (2019-10-02) ### Features - Add PurgeCSS to improve build size even more ### Fixed - Fix minor issue with styles and build ## 8.0.0 (2019-09-19) ### Features - Added 15 design variations (5 layouts with 3 different styles per layout) - Allow Top Navigation to have direct links instead of only dropdowns - Add TailwindCSS for easy customization of Utility CSS ### Fixes - Optimize Paddings/Margins - Optimize RTL - Optimize Styling for Layouts ## 1.0.0 (2019-08-22) ### Features - Initial release

content.text:
# AG Grid Documentation Search Results

**Query:** disable fill handle double click fill down range handle suppress fill handle cellSelection
**Version:** 35.1.0
**Framework:** angular
**Total Results:** 10

---

### suppressClearOnFillReduction

```typescript
/**
 * Set this to `true` to prevent cell values from being cleared when the Range Selection is reduced by the Fill Handle.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.suppressClearOnFillReduction` instead
 */
boolean
```

---

### enableFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to enable the Fill Handle.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle` instead
 */
boolean
```

---

### suppressFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to true to prevent the fillHandle from being rendered in any cell that belongs to this column
 * 
 */
boolean
```

---

### CellSelectionOptions<TData = any>

```typescript
interface CellSelectionOptions<TData = any> {
  suppressMultiRanges?: boolean;
  enableHeaderHighlight?: boolean;
  enableColumnSelection?: boolean;
  handle?: RangeHandleOptions | FillHandleOptions<TData>;
};
```

---

### FillHandleOptions<TData = any>

```typescript
interface FillHandleOptions<TData = any> {
  mode: 'fill';
  suppressClearOnFillReduction?: boolean;
  direction?: 'x' | 'y' | 'xy';
  setFillValue?: <TContext = any>(params: FillOperationParams<TData, TContext>) => any;
};
```

---

### fillOperation

```typescript
/**
 * Callback to fill values instead of simply copying values or increasing number values using linear progression.
 * 
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle.setFillValue` instead
 */
FillOperation<TData>
```

---

### fillHandleDirection

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `'x'` to force the fill handle direction to horizontal, or set to `'y'` to force the fill handle direction to vertical.
 * 
 * @default 'xy'
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle.direction` instead
 */
'x' | 'y' | 'xy'
```

---

### FillEndEvent<TData = any, TContext = any>

```typescript
interface FillEndEvent<TData = any, TContext = any> {
  initialRange: CellRange;
  finalRange: CellRange;
  api: GridApi<TData>;
  context: TContext;
  type: 'fillEnd';
};
```

---

### isSuppressFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Returns `true` if the fill handle is suppressed.
 * 
 */
() => boolean
```

---

### enableRangeHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to enable the Range Handle.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle` instead
 */
boolean
```

payload.message: AG Grid docs confirm three useful pieces: `suppressFillHandle` can hide the fill handle per column, `cellSelection.handle` is the v35 way to control fill/range behavior, and undo requires `undoRedoCellEditing` plus the API button calling `undoCellEditing()`. I’m now reading the actual RFQ grid setup and link renderer path.

content.text: AG Grid docs confirm three useful pieces: `suppressFillHandle` can hide the fill handle per column, `cellSelection.handle` is the v35 way to control fill/range behavior, and undo requires `undoRedoCellEditing` plus the API button calling `undoCellEditing()`. I’m now reading the actual RFQ grid setup and link renderer path.

payload.message: The Tech Pack column is being rendered as a raw anchor string in `rfq.component.ts`, which explains why a normal click immediately navigates. I’m going to replace that with a controlled renderer/edit flow and wire undo through AG Grid’s own undo stack instead of the current custom snapshot code.

content.text: The Tech Pack column is being rendered as a raw anchor string in `rfq.component.ts`, which explains why a normal click immediately navigates. I’m going to replace that with a controlled renderer/edit flow and wire undo through AG Grid’s own undo stack instead of the current custom snapshot code.

payload.message: The current undo button is a homegrown “fetch all rows and restore an old row” path, which will miss fill/copy/paste stacks and can restore stale server data. AG Grid already records edits, fill handle, paste, and delete when `undoRedoCellEditing` is enabled, so I’m going to switch the button to that API and keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged`.

content.text: The current undo button is a homegrown “fetch all rows and restore an old row” path, which will miss fill/copy/paste stacks and can restore stale server data. AG Grid already records edits, fill handle, paste, and delete when `undoRedoCellEditing` is enabled, so I’m going to switch the button to that API and keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged`.

content.text: Before editing, the concrete changes are: make every RFQ column suppress the fill handle, remove `cellSelection.handle` from master/detail grid options, add native AG Grid undo options, change Tech Pack Link into a non-navigating cell renderer with explicit Open/Edit/Upload controls, and make the Tech Pack value setter return `true` so edits enter AG Grid’s undo history.

payload.message:
Implemented in `designflow-frontend` on `sandbox-albert`.

What changed:
- Removed the AG Grid fill handle from RFQ master/detail grids, so the blue-dot double-click fill-down behavior is gone.
- Added defensive `suppressFillHandle: true` to all RFQ column defs, including Tech Pack Link.
- Changed Tech Pack Link from a whole-cell `<a>` into explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` controls. Normal cell clicking no longer navigates away; users can edit via double-click or the `Edit` button.
- Replaced the broken custom undo logic with AG Grid native undo: `undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`, and the toolbar button now calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`.
- Removed the stale “fetch all RFQ rows on every edit” undo bookkeeping.

Key files:
- [rfq.grid.config.ts](C:/repos/dflow%20plm/designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts:16)
- [rfq.component.ts](C:/repos/dflow%20plm/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:291)
- [detail-cell-renderer.component.ts](C:/repos/dflow%20plm/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:55)
- [rfq.component.scss](C:/repos/dflow%20plm/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.scss)

I read the 65 Markdown files, used AG Grid MCP docs pinned to Angular `35.1.0`, and kept the actual work scoped to the RFQ AG Grid path.

Verification:
- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.spec.ts src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.spec.ts --runInBand`
- `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`

Both passed.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

content.text: what's 'fill handle'? i still want to be able to drag-to-copy.

payload.message: what's 'fill handle'? i still want to be able to drag-to-copy.

“Fill handle” is AG Grid’s name for that little blue square/dot on the selected cell. I misunderstood the desired scope: you want the handle kept for drag-copy, but the double-click auto-fill-to-bottom behavior blocked. I’ll switch the implementation to that narrower fix.

content.text: “Fill handle” is AG Grid’s name for that little blue square/dot on the selected cell. I misunderstood the desired scope: you want the handle kept for drag-copy, but the double-click auto-fill-to-bottom behavior blocked. I’ll switch the implementation to that narrower fix.

content.text:
# AG Grid Documentation Search Results

**Query:** fill handle double click copy down disable double click drag fill setFillValue fill start end events
**Version:** 35.1.0
**Framework:** angular
**Total Results:** 10

---

### FillHandleOptions<TData = any>

```typescript
interface FillHandleOptions<TData = any> {
  mode: 'fill';
  suppressClearOnFillReduction?: boolean;
  direction?: 'x' | 'y' | 'xy';
  setFillValue?: <TContext = any>(params: FillOperationParams<TData, TContext>) => any;
};
```

---

### FillStartEvent<TData = any, TContext = any>

```typescript
interface FillStartEvent<TData = any, TContext = any> {
  api: GridApi<TData>;
  context: TContext;
  type: 'fillStart';
};
```

---

### FillEndEvent<TData = any, TContext = any>

```typescript
interface FillEndEvent<TData = any, TContext = any> {
  initialRange: CellRange;
  finalRange: CellRange;
  api: GridApi<TData>;
  context: TContext;
  type: 'fillEnd';
};
```

---

### fillStart

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has started.
 * 
 */
(event: FillStartEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### onFillStart

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has started.
 * 
 */
(event: FillStartEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### fillOperation

```typescript
/**
 * Callback to fill values instead of simply copying values or increasing number values using linear progression.
 * 
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle.setFillValue` instead
 */
FillOperation<TData>
```

---

### enableFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to enable the Fill Handle.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle` instead
 */
boolean
```

---

### onFillEnd

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has ended.
 * 
 */
(event: FillEndEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### fillEnd

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has ended.
 * 
 */
(event: FillEndEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### suppressFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to true to prevent the fillHandle from being rendered in any cell that belongs to this column
 * 
 */
boolean
```

(event: FillStartEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### fillOperation

```typescript
/**
 * Callback to fill values instead of simply copying values or increasing number values using linear progression.
 * 
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle.setFillValue` instead
 */
FillOperation<TData>
```

---

### enableFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to `true` to enable the Fill Handle.
 * 
 * @default false
 * @deprecated v32.2 Use `cellSelection.handle` instead
 */
boolean
```

---

### onFillEnd

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has ended.
 * 
 */
(event: FillEndEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### fillEnd

```typescript
/**
 * Fill operation has ended.
 * 
 */
(event: FillEndEvent<TData>) => void
```

---

### suppressFillHandle

```typescript
/**
 * Set to true to prevent the fillHandle from being rendered in any cell that belongs to this column
 * 
 */
boolean
```"}]

payload.message: AG Grid docs don’t expose a “disable only double-click fill” option; they expose the fill handle as one feature. So the practical fix is to keep AG Grid’s fill handle enabled, and intercept `dblclick` on the handle element before AG Grid turns it into copy-to-bottom.

payload.message:
“Fill handle” is AG Grid’s name for the little blue dot/square at the bottom-right of the selected cell.

You’re right: removing it also removes drag-to-copy. I corrected that.

Current behavior now:
- Drag-to-copy stays enabled.
- Double-clicking the blue dot is blocked before AG Grid can auto-fill down the whole grid.
- Tech Pack Link still has explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` controls.
- Undo uses AG Grid native undo, so edits/fill/paste should participate in the undo stack.

Verified again:
- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath ... --runInBand`
- `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`

Both passed.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

content.text:
Update only the Markdown files that need durable knowledge from this specific session.

## Goal

Record what this session learned or changed so future developers and future AI sessions do not have to rediscover it.

Focus on:
- Information from this session that will still matter later.
- Idiosyncrasies that may look wrong but are intentional.
- Mistakes, dead ends, or assumptions from this session that future sessions should avoid.
- Architecture, data flow, deployment, configuration, or workflow changes.
- New operational steps, rollback notes, feature flags, identifiers, limits, or dependencies.
- Any temporary/incomplete work that needs a handoff.

## Rules

- Derive every doc update from code, config, migrations, workflows, commands run, or verified session findings.
- Do not document guesses as facts. Mark unknowns clearly and say how to verify them.
- Do not add secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or credential values.
- Do not duplicate large explanations across multiple docs. Put the short routing/context note in `AGENTS.md` only if future AI sessions need to see it early; put topic detail in the relevant doc under `docs/`.
- If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.

## What To Update

- `AGENTS.md`: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.
- `HANDOFF.md`: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.
- Topic docs under `docs/`: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.
- `README.md`: update only if the quick-start or top-level orientation changed.
- `CLAUDE.md`: update only for Claude Code-specific workflow rules. General AI/developer guidance belongs in `AGENTS.md`.

## Required Session Note Shape

When adding session-derived knowledge, prefer concise entries like:

```md
### [Short decision or warning name]

What changed:
[One or two sentences.]

Why:
[Constraint, incident, bug, user decision, or operational reason.]

Future sessions should:
[Concrete instruction: what to do, what to avoid, where to verify.]
```

For unfinished work, include:

```md
## [Workstream name]

Status:
[done / partial / blocked / not started]

Done:
[Verified completed items.]

Next action:
[Exact next step.]

Risks / watchouts:
[Anything future sessions must not miss.]
```

## Verification Before Final Response

- Docs changed are limited to files that actually needed updates.
- New claims match the repository or verified session findings.
- No secrets were added.
- `HANDOFF.md` exists only if continuation context is needed.
- Any new quirk or warning explains why it exists and what would break if ignored.

## Final Report

Reply with:

```md
## Documentation Updates
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `path/to/file.md` | Short summary |

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: present/absent
- Reason: ...

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: yes/no
- Secrets added: yes/no
- Unknowns remaining: ...
```

src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:81:## AG Grid patterns specific to this module
src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:85:Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:88:- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)
src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:111:Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.
src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:121:Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

src\app\pages\rfq\README.md:130:| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |

docs\architecture.md:178:## AG Grid patterns
docs\architecture.md:180:All feature grids use AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1 with the client-side row model (CSRM).
docs\architecture.md:192:RFQ and Production Tracking use AG Grid master-detail. The master row is an RFQ item; the detail row is vendor quotes. The `DetailCellRenderer` component manages the detail grid independently, with its own column definitions loaded from the backend.

AGENTS.md:9:Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes.

AGENTS.md:414:**Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value.

AGENTS.md:523:## 14. Critical incident log
AGENTS.md:527:**What happened:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to the Date Modified column (commit `43db88d`) activated AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline. All group rows lost `params.data` (became `undefined`) in every valueSetter and valueGetter.
AGENTS.md:531:**Root cause:** AG Grid Enterprise activates aggregation for the entire grid (not just the column) when any column has an `aggFunc`. Group rows then use aggregated values, not `params.data`.
AGENTS.md:535:**Rule added:** Never add `aggFunc` to a column in the RFQ grid unless you have confirmed that all valueSetters and valueGetters gracefully handle `params.data === undefined` for group rows.
AGENTS.md:541:**What happened:** Any value typed into CBM/pc (or any column calling `recalculate_Lic_Gen`) disappeared after pressing Enter. The AG Grid cell reverted to empty.
AGENTS.md:543:**Root cause (primary):** All 16 margin setter functions in `equation.ts` accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId`. In AG Grid v35, `userProvidedColDef` is `undefined`, so every call threw a `TypeError`. Because the valueSetter is `async`, AG Grid v35 awaited the rejected Promise and cancelled the edit.
AGENTS.md:549:**Rule added:** All AG Grid API references must use the documented public API. `userProvidedColDef`, `columnApi`, and `getRowNodeId` are removed in v31–v35. Check `AGENTS.md §11` before using any AG Grid internal property.
AGENTS.md:557:**Root cause:** AG Grid v35 infers `cellDataType` from the existing data in each row. The backend stores CBM/pc values as strings (e.g. `"0.05"`), so AG Grid inferred the column type as `'text'`. The `numericCellEditor.getValue()` returns a JS `number`. AG Grid v35's type-check gate runs **before** the valueSetter — it saw `number` vs. inferred `text`, issued warning `#135` (`Data type of the new value does not match the cell data type of the column`), and silently reverted the edit without ever calling the valueSetter.
AGENTS.md:559:**Recovery:** Set `cellDataType: false` on the `cbm_pc` column definition in `rfq.component.ts` inside `getAllRFQItem()`. This disables AG Grid's type inference for that column. The `numericCellEditor` already prevents non-numeric input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so type enforcement is not lost.
AGENTS.md:561:**Why not `cellDataType: 'number'`:** Setting `'number'` causes AG Grid to apply its built-in number formatter to all existing cells. Because existing values are strings, every cell displayed "invalid number". `false` is correct.
AGENTS.md:563:**Diagnostic signal:** AG Grid warning `#135` in the browser console. If a cell edit disappears and you do NOT see any `[valueSetter]` log lines, this warning is the thing to look for — the valueSetter was never called.
AGENTS.md:573:**Root cause:** The License column has a `valueSetter` dynamically added in `rfq.component.ts` (~line 1024) that only accepts `params.newValue` if `params.newValue.title` is truthy — it stores the value internally as `{title: "..."}` so the paired `valueGetter` can read `.title` for display. The custom cell editor's `getValue()` was returning a plain string (e.g. `"Disney"`), causing the valueSetter to evaluate `"Disney".title === undefined` → falsy → return `undefined`. AG Grid treated the edit as unchanged, never updated `rowNode.data`, and never fired `onCellValueChanged`.
AGENTS.md:624:**Rule D — Verify spec assertion values against source before committing.** After any change that removes or renames a colDef/gridOptions property: (1) grep the spec files for the property name, (2) trace the actual runtime value from the source (including defaults/fallbacks), (3) confirm the assertion matches. Do not assume the value will be `undefined` just because an override was deleted.
AGENTS.md:653:**Rule C — Do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]` on `mat-select`.** `[formControl]` and `[(value)]` are from two different forms APIs and both try to own the value. Using both together produces undefined behavior. Pick one; prefer `[formControl]` for reactive forms.
AGENTS.md:673:**What happened:** The multi-select cell editors for License and Div columns in the RFQ grid were unreliable: the panel sometimes didn't open on click, and when it did open, clicking outside to dismiss it didn't work (had to press ESC).
AGENTS.md:703:**Recovery:** Updated `CORE_BACKEND_URL` in all three BFF Cloud Run service configurations to the `.run.app` service URL. Also updated `designflow-bff/env/.env.*` to match. Full gcloud commands recorded in `docs/deployment.md` § 2026-05-26 SSO 401 incident.
AGENTS.md:772:**Fix:** `suggestCrossTerms()` — a cheap no-reasoning AI call that returns 3–5 suggested search terms. The AI identifies which words are classification-relevant (material, product type) vs. incidental (qualifiers, attributes). It also suggests alternate spellings CBP rulings commonly use (hyphenated → unhyphenated: "anti-fatigue" → "antifatigue", "anti fatigue").
AGENTS.md:782:**Root cause:** The sort (`sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator`) was set only on the column with colId `quote_price`. The detail grid loads its columns from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), and the live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under the **legacy colId `price`**, not `quote_price`. The sort landed on a non-existent column, so AG Grid applied no price sort and `Name`'s `colDef.sort` kept winning.

# RFQ Module

This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.

---

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~3600 lines. |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |

Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder.

---

## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`

Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.

- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.

In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.

---

## Calculation pipeline

**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**

### Recalculation trigger chain

When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:

```
calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
recalculate_Lic_Gen()
  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
```

All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.

---

## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips

The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).

The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.

---

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last

`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).

A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.

**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.

### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard

Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:

- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.

The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.

### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)

When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:

- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).

The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.

---

## Window globals (known tech debt)

`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.

Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**

Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
- `src/app/pages/ui/` (component showcase)
- `src/app/pages/documentation/`
- `src/app/pages/pages/` (pricing, faq, guides, invoice)

---

## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions

### Angular project name is "vex"

**Looks like:** The wrong project name is configured — `angular.json` names the project `vex`.

**Actually:** Designflow was bootstrapped from the commercial Vex Angular template. The CLI project name `vex` is the template's name and has never been changed.

**Why:** Renaming an Angular CLI project requires updating `angular.json`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and several other files. The risk of breakage during rename outweighs the cosmetic benefit.

**Do not change because:** Build scripts in `package.json` (`ng serve`, `ng build`) rely on the `vex` project name implicitly through Angular CLI defaults. Renaming without updating every reference would break the build.

---

### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering

**Looks like:** Date Modified uses a custom comparator and `postSortRows` hook but no `aggFunc` — looks like incomplete sorting implementation.

**Actually:** This is deliberate. Adding `aggFunc` to ANY column activates AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline for all group rows, causing `params.data` to be `undefined` in every `valueGetter` and `valueSetter` callback on group rows.

**Why:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to Date Modified (commit `43db88d`) broke all landed-cost and margin calculations because group rows suddenly had no `params.data`. Removed in `0a2f4f7`.

**Do not change because:** Restoring `aggFunc` on any column will silently break every calculation that reads `params.data` in a valueSetter.

---

### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally)

**Looks like:** A typo — should be `getMasterFieldName`.

**Actually:** The function is named `getMasterFiledName` (not `FieldName`) across `rfq.component.ts`, `equation.ts`, `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`, and related files. It is the stable canonical function name used throughout the codebase.

**Why:** Renaming it now would require touching dozens of call-sites across multiple files, with no functional benefit and real risk of missed replacements.

**Do not change because:** Renaming half the occurrences would break the build; renaming all of them is a pure cosmetic refactor with zero risk benefit.

---

### Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR as a raw number

**Looks like:** Inconsistent data handling — some places access `duty_rate.title`, others use the value directly.

**Actually:** The duty rate field is stored on the row as an object `{ title: "7.5" }` when set via the autocomplete editor, but can also be a raw string or number in some data paths.

**Why:** The backend returns it as an object with a label; older code accessed `.title` directly without guarding.

**Do not change because:** The guard pattern `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` handles both forms. Changing to always-object or always-primitive would require a coordinated backend change.

---

### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts

**Looks like:** Anti-pattern — using the global `window` object to access an Angular service.

**Actually:** Some AG Grid callback closures registered in `applyColumnDefinitions()` (which runs before the component is fully initialized) cannot close over `this` because the method that creates them is called from a context where `this` bindings differ.

**Why:** This is a known tech-debt pattern from the original codebase. The `window['equation']`, `window['colHeaders']`, and `window['_snackBar']` globals inside `container_price` were cleaned up in commit `8fa013a`. The remaining `window['itemService']` references in toolbar button callbacks have the same root cause.

**Do not change without understanding:** Moving these calls to `this.itemService` will throw `Cannot read property of undefined` if the closure is created before Angular DI is complete.

---

### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services

**Looks like:** Unnecessary over-engineering — the calculation functions could live directly in the grid components.

**Actually:** Each tracking module (RFQ, production, licensing) has its own `equation.ts` calculation service. This is intentional separation: the grid component handles AG Grid wiring, the equation service handles all business math.

**Why:** The calculation chains (8 margin × 2 type = 16 setters) are called from multiple places (direct cell edits, bulk updates, container cost dialog). Keeping them in an injectable service allows them to be called from dialogs as well as the grid component.

**Do not change because:** Inlining the math into the component would make it impossible to call from dialogs without circular dependencies.

---

### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)

**Looks like:** Redundant — why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition?

**Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`.

**Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access.

**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.

---

### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total

**Looks like:** Redundant storage — the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`).

**Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style).

**Where it lives:**
- `[duty_rate field].title` — the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`.
- `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` — the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend.
- `[duty_rate field]_equation` — ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`.

**Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read.

**Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates — they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit).

---

### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35)

**Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value.

**Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value.

**Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`.

**Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs.

---

### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip`

**Looks like:** Inconsistent — the 8 `*_price_sales` columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer` and set a plain browser `title`, while every other column uses the styled `CustomTooltipComponent`.

**Actually:** Those 8 columns must show their duty-rate/container-price snapshot tooltip **always on hover**. The grid sets `tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated'` globally, and AG Grid has **no per-column override** for that mode.

**Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up.

**Do not change because:** Reverting to the shared tooltip silently re-gates these tooltips behind truncation (they would rarely show, since the values are short currency strings). Capture lives in `onCellValueChanged` → `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()`; rendering in `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/`.

---

### Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false`

**Looks like:** A new `@Component` decorator without `standalone` works in Angular 15 but silently fails in Angular 21 when declared in an `NgModule`.

**Actually:** Angular 21 changed the `@Component` decorator default from `standalone: false` (Angular 14–16) to `standalone: true`. Any component you add to an `NgModule`'s `declarations` array will throw `NG0302: 'app-xyz' is not a known element` at runtime unless `standalone: false` is explicitly set.

**Why:** The entire app is still module-based (not standalone). We have not migrated `AppModule`, `RFQModule`, etc. to standalone mode.

**Do not change because:** Removing `standalone: false` from a module-declared component will break the module at runtime. Add it to every new `@Component` in this codebase until a full standalone migration is planned and coordinated. Example: `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` required `standalone: false`.

---

### Vendor-facing UI strings are hardcoded bilingual (`中文 - English`), not i18n keys

**Looks like:** Untranslated, hardcoded strings like `我不想给价格 - Decline`, `保留价格 - Keep Price`, and the `报价 - Quoted <date>` re-quote tooltip — looks like someone forgot to use the translation framework.

**Actually:** `@ngx-translate/core` is installed but **not wired** (no `assets/i18n/*.json`, no `TranslateService` init). The vendor portal is used by Chinese factories, so the handful of vendor-facing strings are written inline as `中文 - English` directly in the templates/colDefs. This is the established pattern (`rfq-button.component.html` shipped the Decline button this way first).

**Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention.

---

### Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for the factory, blocked for staff

**Looks like:** Nothing stops the same factory being attached to an RFQ item twice — there is no DB unique constraint on `(RFQitem, vendor)` and no backend duplicate check, and the detail sub-grid even has an "Add Quote" button that adds a second row for a factory that is already present.

**Actually:** Two flows with deliberately opposite rules. A **factory** may legitimately submit a second quote line for the same item (different price/MOQ, with a note explaining the difference) via "Add Quote" — that is real data and is allowed. **Staff** (sourcing/production/admin) must NOT add the same factory twice via the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog. The dialog (`src/app/pages/rfq/vendor/vendor.component.ts`) renders already-attached factories as red, unclickable chips: `toggleSelection()` early-returns for attached IDs, the group "All" checkbox skips them, and the chips get the `vendor-already-added` class. The attached IDs are seeded from `DialogData.vendors`, which `detail-cell-renderer.onBtnNewVendor()` builds from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)`.

**Why:** A factory quoting twice is a valid business event; staff adding the same factory twice is an accident that produces confusing duplicate quote rows.

**Do not change because:** The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB or backend constraint behind it, so the Select Factories dialog is the only thing preventing accidental staff-side duplicates. It depends on the master row property being capital `Vendors` with elements shaped `{ Vendor: { id } }`; changing that casing (e.g. to `vendors`) makes `existingVendorIds` always empty and silently disables the guard. The factory-side "Add Quote" path must stay unguarded.

---

### Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one

**Looks like:** `applyDefaultPriceSort()` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` searches `colHeaders` for a column whose `colId` is `'quote_price'` **or** `'price'` (preferring the visible one) before wiring the default sort — looks like defensive over-engineering that could be simplified to a single hardcoded colId.

### What it does

Nothing fires `createNotification()` on business events. The notification system exists
(routes, service, frontend bell/badge) but no triggers have been wired.

setImmediate(async () => {
  try {

      event: 'assigned',
      title: 'Task assigned',

});

the model falls back on training data. A simple keyword search would inject the exact USITC

- Frontend: `ai-settings.service.ts` — add `searchHtsRates(desc: string)` method

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

 ### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35)
@@
 **Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs.
 
 ---
 
+### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down
+
+**Looks like:** The RFQ grid still enables AG Grid's fill handle (`cellSelection.handle.mode = 'fill'`), even though double-clicking that blue handle previously copied a Tech Pack Link down the whole grid.
+
+**Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut.
+
+**Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`.
+
+**Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker.
+
+---
+
 ### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip`
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch

@@
-| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~3600 lines. |
+| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
@@
 ### Async valueSetters
@@
 - Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
 - Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)
+
+### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down
+
+What changed:
+The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.
+
+Why:
+Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.
+
+### Tech Pack Link editing and opening
+
+What changed:
+The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.
+
+Why:
+When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.
+
+### Native AG Grid undo
+
+What changed:
+RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.
+
+Why:
+AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.
 
 ### Column identity: never hardcode field names
*** End Patch

---
name: "yeet"
description: "Publish local changes to GitHub by confirming scope, committing intentionally, pushing the branch, and opening a draft PR through the GitHub app from this plugin, with `gh` used only as a fallback where connector coverage is insufficient."
---

# GitHub Publish Changes

## Overview

Use this skill only when the user explicitly wants the full publish flow from the local checkout: branch setup if needed, staging, commit, push, and opening a pull request.

This workflow is hybrid:

- Use local `git` for branch creation, staging, commit, and push.
- Prefer the GitHub app from this plugin for pull request creation after the branch is on the remote.
- Use `gh` as a fallback for current-branch PR discovery, auth checks, or PR creation when the connector path cannot infer the repository or head branch cleanly.

## Prerequisites

- Require GitHub CLI `gh`. Check `gh --version`. If missing, ask the user to install `gh` and stop.
- Require authenticated `gh` session. Run `gh auth status`. If not authenticated, ask the user to run `gh auth login` (and re-run `gh auth status`) before continuing.
- Require a local git repository with a clean understanding of which changes belong in the PR.

## Naming conventions

- Branch: `codex/{description}` when starting from main/master/default.
- Commit: `{description}` (terse).
- PR title: `[codex] {description}` summarizing the full diff.

## Workflow

1. Confirm intended scope.
   - Run `git status -sb` and inspect the diff before staging.
   - If the working tree contains unrelated changes, do not default to `git add -A`. Ask the user which files belong in the PR.
2. Determine the branch strategy.
   - If on `main`, `master`, or another default branch, create `codex/{description}`.
   - Otherwise stay on the current branch.
3. Stage only the intended changes.
   - Prefer explicit file paths when the worktree is mixed.
   - Use `git add -A` only when the user has confirmed the whole worktree belongs in scope.
4. Commit tersely with the confirmed description.
5. Run the most relevant checks available if they have not already been run.
   - If checks fail due to missing dependencies or tools, install what is needed and rerun once.
6. Push with tracking: `git push -u origin $(git branch --show-current)`.
7. Open a draft PR.
   - Prefer the GitHub app from this plugin for PR creation after the push succeeds.
   - Derive `repository_full_name` from the remote, for example by normalizing `git remote get-url origin` or by using `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner`.
   - Derive `head_branch` from `git branch --show-current`.
   - Derive `base_branch` from the user request when specified; otherwise use the remote default branch, for example via `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef`.
   - If the branch is being pushed from a fork or the PR target differs from the remote that was just pushed, prefer `gh pr create` fallback because the connector PR creation flow expects one repository target and may not encode cross-repo head semantics cleanly.
   - If connector-based PR creation cannot infer the repository or branch cleanly, fall back to `gh pr create --draft --fill --head $(git branch --show-current)`.
   - Write the PR body to a temp file with real newlines when using CLI fallback so the markdown renders cleanly.
8. Summarize the result with branch name, commit, PR target, validation, and anything the user still needs to confirm.

## Write Safety

- Never stage unrelated user changes silently.
- Never push without confirming scope when the worktree is mixed.
- Default to a draft PR unless the user explicitly asks for a ready-for-review PR.
- If the repository does not appear to be connected to an accessible GitHub remote, stop and explain the blocker before making assumptions.

## PR Body Expectations

The PR description should use real Markdown prose and cover:

- what changed
- why it changed
- the user or developer impact
- the root cause when the PR is a fix
- the checks used to validate it

{"command":"$body = @'
## Summary
- keep RFQ fill-handle drag-copy enabled while blocking the dangerous double-click fill-down shortcut
- replace Tech Pack Link's whole-cell anchor with explicit Edit, Open, and Upload controls
- switch the RFQ toolbar Undo button to AG Grid native undo and document the AG Grid/RFQ quirks

## Why
A Tech Pack Link cell fill-handle double-click can copy one URL down the displayed grid and destroy row-specific links. Users still need drag-to-copy, so the fix blocks only the double-click event on `.ag-fill-handle` and keeps normal fill-handle drag behavior.

content.text:
explain to me how the undo function works: what does it do, what doesn't it do, how does it do it?
"Tech Pack Link still has explicit Edit, Open, and Upload controls." - where do i see those controls?

payload.message:
explain to me how the undo function works: what does it do, what doesn't it do, how does it do it?
"Tech Pack Link still has explicit Edit, Open, and Upload controls." - where do i see those controls?

The RFQ Undo button now calls AG Grid’s built-in undo stack:

```ts
undo() {
  if (!this.gridApi) return;
  this.gridApi.undoCellEditing();
}
```

What it does:
- Undoes the last cell edit that AG Grid knows about.
- Covers normal cell edits, paste, delete/clear, fill-handle drag, and undoable AG Grid edit operations.
- Fires AG Grid `cellValueChanged` for the reverted value, so our existing `onCellValueChanged()` persists the reverted row back to the backend with `updateRfqItem()`.

What it does not do:
- It is not a database history rollback.
- It does not restore arbitrary previous server state.
- It does not undo row deletes, archives, dialog actions, adding factories, sending reminders, etc.
- It only works for edits that went through AG Grid’s editing/value-change pipeline.
- If you refresh the page, the in-memory AG Grid undo stack is gone.

How it works:
- AG Grid records cell edits internally when `undoRedoCellEditing: true`.
- We set the stack limit to `50`.
- The toolbar Undo button invokes `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`.
- AG Grid reverts the cell value and emits `cellValueChanged`.
- Our existing save handler persists that reverted row.

For the Tech Pack controls: they appear inside the `Tech Pack Link` cell itself after this change. Instead of the whole cell being a clickable URL, the cell should show the URL text plus three small buttons: `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload`.

- `Edit`: starts AG Grid text editing for the URL.
- `Open`: opens the URL in a new tab.
- `Upload`: opens the existing upload dialog and writes the returned shared link into the cell.

If you do not see those buttons in the running app, the deployed/build version you’re looking at likely does not include this branch/commit yet, or that column is too narrow and needs widening/scrolling.

content.text: Tech Pack controls: so if a user (like Vendor/Factory) doesn't have edit permissions on that cell, those buttons don't appear at all, right?

payload.message: Tech Pack controls: so if a user (like Vendor/Factory) doesn't have edit permissions on that cell, those buttons don't appear at all, right?

No, not as currently committed.

Right now `renderTechPackCell()` always renders `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` for the Tech Pack Link cell. It does not yet check whether the current user/role can edit that column.

Also, the column config currently includes `vendor` in the Tech Pack Link roles and inherits `editable: true`, so Vendor/Factory users likely can see and use those controls unless another runtime permission layer changes that later.

The safer intended behavior should be:

- Read-only user: show URL text + `Open` only.
- Editable user: show `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload`.
- Empty value + read-only user: probably show `No link` and no buttons.

So your instinct is right about what the UX should be, but the current implementation needs one more permission gate to make that true.

Done. Tech Pack controls now respect the cell’s AG Grid `editable` permission.

Behavior now:
- Editable cell: shows `Edit`, `Open` if there is a link, and `Upload`.
- Read-only cell with a link: shows only `Open`.
- Read-only empty cell: shows `No link` and no buttons.

I added tests for editable, read-only-with-link, and read-only-empty cases.

Pushed follow-up commit:
`187ea249 fix(rfq): gate tech pack edit controls`

PR is updated:
[PR #115: Fix RFQ Tech Pack fill-handle safety](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/115)

Validation:
- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts --runInBand`
- `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`

Working tree is clean.
